questions.questionText: ICD-11 and DSM-5 are classification systems for mental disorders published by:
questions.optionB: ICD-11 by WHO (International Classification of Diseases) and DSM-5 by APA (American Psychiatric Association) — both multiaxial diagnostic systems
questions.explanation: ICD-11 (WHO, 2022): international standard for health conditions including mental disorders, used worldwide. DSM-5 (APA, 2013): primarily used in USA, gold standard for psychiatric research and clinical practice. Both classify mental disorders based on symptom clusters, duration, and functional impairment. Key categories: neurodevelopmental, schizophrenia spectrum, bipolar, depressive, anxiety, OCD, trauma/stress-related, substance use, personality, neurocognitive disorders. Reference: Niraj Ahuja — Psychiatric Classification.
